request_for_records_under_the_common_law.interest_in_subject_matter:
I am requesting access to records concerning the award, administration, and performance of the 2022–2024 solid waste collection and recycling contract between the Township of Maplewood and Garden State Environmental Services, Inc., including the original bid submissions, scoring sheets, correspondence regarding performance issues, and notices of any contract amendments or change orders.

I am a resident and taxpayer of the Township of Maplewood, residing at 184 Oak Terrace, Maplewood, NJ 07040, and I have lived in the township since 2013. Over the last two years, my neighborhood has experienced repeated and significant disruptions in trash and recycling collection—missed pick‑ups for entire blocks, inconsistent recycling collection, and instances where trash was left out for multiple days, leading to rodent issues and strong odors, especially in the summer months. I have personally reported missed collections to the Department of Public Works on at least six occasions between July 2023 and May 2024, usually by phone and once via the township’s online service request portal.

In addition to the inconvenience and health concerns, I have a direct financial interest in how this contract is being managed, because the cost of the solid waste contract is funded through local property taxes and municipal fees that I pay each quarter on my home. My most recent 2024 property tax bill specifically references increases tied to rising public works and sanitation costs. As a homeowner with a fixed-rate mortgage and two children in the Maplewood–South Orange school district, I am very conscious of how municipal spending decisions affect both my monthly budget and the long‑term value of my property.

Over the past year, I have attended several Township Committee meetings—specifically on 2023-09-19, 2024-01-16, and 2024-04-09—where residents raised questions about the quality of trash collection and whether the contractor was meeting its obligations. During the public comment period at the 2024-01-16 meeting, I personally spoke and described missed collections on Oak Terrace and the adjoining streets (Elmwood Avenue and Ridgewood Road). In response, a committee member indicated that the township was ‘reviewing the contract and monitoring performance,’ but did not provide details. The meeting minutes and video reflect that discussion.

I am also an active member of the Oak Terrace Neighborhood Association, an informal group of roughly 25 households that communicates primarily through an email list and a group text thread. I have volunteered to gather factual information about the underlying waste collection contract so that our group can better understand whether the township is enforcing performance standards, whether penalties have been assessed for repeated failures, and whether there are any plans to rebid or renegotiate the contract. Several neighbors are elderly or have mobility issues, and missed trash pick‑ups create particular hardship for them. Because I am relatively comfortable reading contracts and basic financial information—I work as an operations manager for a mid‑sized logistics company in Newark—I agreed to take the lead on requesting and reviewing these records.

My legally recognized interest under the common law right of access is twofold:

1. **Taxpayer and Resident Oversight Interest**: As a taxpayer contributing to the funding of this contract, I have a legitimate interest in determining whether public funds are being spent efficiently and in accordance with the terms approved by the Township Committee. I want to know, for example, whether the contractor bid significantly lower than competing vendors and whether that lower price might be connected to the service problems we are now experiencing. I am also interested in whether the township has documented service failures and, if so, whether it has invoked any contractual remedies (such as liquidated damages, withholding of payments, or formal notices to cure). This directly affects my pocketbook and my family’s quality of life.

2. **Health, Safety, and Neighborhood Quality Interest**: Repeated missed trash and recycling collections have led to overflowing cans, bags being torn open by animals, and an increase in visible rodents around our block. My younger child has mild asthma, and we have noticed that the odor and debris are worse during hot and humid periods. I therefore have a concrete personal and familial interest in understanding whether the township is holding the contractor to sanitary and timeliness standards designed to protect public health and maintain basic neighborhood cleanliness.

I do not seek these records for commercial resale or for any competitive business advantage. My intention is to review the contract terms, performance documentation, and related correspondence in order to:
- Share a clear, factual summary with my neighbors via our association email list;
- Decide whether to advocate at future Township Committee meetings for stricter enforcement of the current contract or for a rebidding process;
- Determine whether it is appropriate to submit written comments or a petition regarding sanitation services as part of the next municipal budget hearing; and
- Evaluate whether the township’s public descriptions of the contract and its enforcement match what is reflected in the underlying records.

Because the records I am requesting are core municipal documents—bids, contracts, performance reports, and related correspondence prepared or received by public officials in the course of administering a public contract—they clearly qualify as public records under the common law definition. My status as a resident, property owner, and taxpayer directly affected by the quality and cost of these services gives me a specific, legally recognized interest that goes beyond mere general curiosity. I am seeking this information solely to better understand and, if necessary, advocate for improvements in the delivery of a basic municipal service that impacts my household and my immediate community on a weekly basis.
